sources.quotedText: (152) Tahoe Lake and tributaries (Placer and El Dorado Cos.). (A) Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout. (B) Tahoe Lake within 300 feet of the mouth of its tributaries. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout.

sources.title: Cal. Code Regs. Tit. 14, § 7.50(a)(1) — General Provisions (waters listed in subsection (b) closed except during the open season shown)
sources.quotedText: Every body of water listed in subsection (b) is closed to all fishing except during the open season as shown.

sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":268070,"gaz_name":"Tallac Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"El Dorado","objectid_1":1402154}; geometry multipoint, first point (Web Mercator -13364885.3705, 4713227.3011) converts to lon -120.058808, lat 38.941024, near this creek's mouth at Lake Tahoe close to Baldwin Beach (South Lake Tahoe); second point (Web Mercator -13368761.0813, 4709186.1767 → lon -120.093624, lat 38.912783) sits upstream toward Mount Tallac. Corrects prior file coordinates (-120.07847, 38.91985), which were an unsourced midpoint estimate ~2.9 km from this GNIS-recorded mouth point.

groups.humanSummary: Tallac Creek is a south-shore Lake Tahoe tributary — it starts on Mount Tallac and flows north into Lake Tahoe near Baldwin Beach / the Tallac Historic Site (El Dorado Co.). It is not listed under its own name, but as a Tahoe Lake tributary it is governed by the special Tahoe-tributary regulation §7.50(b)(152)(A): catch-and-release open season Sat. before Memorial Day–Sep. 30, artificial lures with barbless hooks only, 0 trout; closed the remainder of the year per §7.50(a)(1). No lake lies on Tallac Creek's main stem, so 'upstream to the first lake' covers the entire creek.
groups.verbatimText: (152) Tahoe Lake and tributaries (Placer and El Dorado Cos.). (A) Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout. (B) Tahoe Lake within 300 feet of the mouth of its tributaries. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout.

parameters.note: Tallac Creek is a tributary of Lake Tahoe (it starts on Mount Tallac and flows north into Lake Tahoe near Baldwin Beach, El Dorado Co.). It is not listed by its own name in §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), but it falls under the Lake Tahoe tributaries entry §7.50(b)(152)(A), which applies to 'Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake' — here the entire creek, since no lake lies on the main stem. Note: the separately catalogued Tallac Lake (a small alpine lake on the north slope of Mount Tallac) is not on this creek's stem; it sits in the adjacent Taylor Creek / Fallen Leaf Lake drainage. Per §7.50(a)(1), waters listed in subsection (b) are closed to all fishing except during the shown open season, so the creek is closed Oct. 1 through the Friday preceding the Saturday before Memorial Day. Date_spec encodes 'Saturday preceding Memorial Day' as the last Monday of May (Memorial Day) minus 2 days, since the resolver has no anchor_holiday implementation.

regulations.humanSummary: Open catch-and-release only, Saturday before Memorial Day through Sept. 30; closed to all fishing the rest of the year (Lake Tahoe tributary special regulation).
regulations.verbatimText: Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout.

regulations.humanSummary: Artificial lures with barbless hooks only during the open window; no bait.
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license. Tallac Creek is wholly within California (El Dorado Co.); no Nevada reciprocity applies to the creek.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
